steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The question asks for the main purpose of the passage. Every sentence describes Polly's internal reactions — her fear, her disinterest, her shock and puzzlement — so the purpose must be about portraying Polly's perspective toward Fanny's friends.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — "To portray Polly's reaction to Fanny's friends": The passage details exactly this: Polly was afraid of them, found their topics uninteresting, and was shocked and puzzled. Every sentence supports a focus on Polly's feelings. ✓
  • Choice B — "To identify the topics Polly talks about with Fanny's friends": The passage says Fanny's friends "talked about things of which she knew nothing" but never names those topics. No specific topics are identified. ✗
  • Choice C — "To explain how Fanny met some of her friends": The passage never mentions how Fanny met her friends. This information is entirely absent. ✗
  • Choice D — "To illustrate how Fanny's friends feel about Polly": The passage focuses on Polly's feelings, not the friends' feelings about Polly. The friends' perspective is never given. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
